brief_notes: Excellent clarity in presenting the paradox of high individual mobility vs population-level genetic stability in Europe over 3000 years. 204 ancient genomes including first data from France and Armenia. Strong quantitative framework with Mantel tests (r=0.78-0.83) and low within-region Fst values (0.002-0.007). Strengths: clear narrative structure, important conceptual resolution of mobility vs stability paradox, well-contextualized against prehistoric turnovers, accessible writing. Weaknesses: no figures (PCA plots would be essential), ADMIXTURE results mentioned in methods but not shown, sample sizes per region-period are small, authentication/quality metrics not shown in results, limited discussion of ascertainment bias. The 6.9% outlier rate is interesting but based on only 14 individuals.
